commands.name: Go — top-level report
commands.description: The single idempotent top-level report. Checks currency (free when no commits landed), offers to re-run checks invalidated by recent commits, refreshes `root`, and prints the narrative. The dashboard you want to live in day-to-day.

commands.name: Fast change-local loop
commands.description: The PR-time quick loop: lint + build + fast tests + canaries. Cheap; run it while working in a change. Then roll up with `verifier:fast:rollup` (validation.pr).

commands.name: Refresh the rollup report
commands.description: Recompute the `root` narrative from the latest child results (cheap — usually no model call). Prints the stored dashboard without forcing new runs.

commands.name: Deep cadence — local destructive + E2E
commands.description: Nightly/CI-grade local proof that the stack really boots. Runs the guarded checks: stack.fresh-seeded (wipes + reseeds local dev data), restart-consistency, artifact.ipfs-domain-smoke, stack.user-journeys (Playwright round-trips), operations.indexer-lag — then refreshes deployment-depth + facet.functionality so the dashboard retains a fresh end-to-end boot proof. Needs ~20 min and the local Docker stack.

commands.name: Deep cadence — full (+ testnet)
commands.description: The deep cadence plus read-only testnet smoke and (with a funded verifier wallet) onchain-to-indexer + website-journey mutation checks. Use only with provisioned verifier/testnet credentials.

commands.name: Refresh functionality facet
commands.description: Re-run the functionality facet (does it work?) — fast loop, full suite, guarded deep-stack/testnet checks, operations canaries.

commands.name: Refresh docs facet
commands.description: Re-run the docs facet — coherence judgment plus the deterministic broken-ref scan.

commands.name: Refresh product facet
commands.description: Re-run the product facet — messaging, workflow-clarity, and manual attestations (is it compelling and usable?).

commands.name: Refresh security facet
commands.description: Re-run the security facet — Hardhat tests, Slither, contract review (is the on-chain surface sound?).

commands.name: Start the scheduler (long-running)
commands.description: The due-only scheduler daemon — auto-runs cheap operational checks (liveness/flakiness/coverage/staleness/known-bad) and folds onInputChange supervisors. Intended for a real process supervisor + external heartbeat cron, not a quick look.

commands.name: Wipe + reseed local dev data
commands.description: Commonality dev helper: stop services, wipe ./data, restart Docker stack, seed. Use when you want a clean local world outside the verifier harness.
